result.instructions: Meridian Paper Wiki exposes two workflows: Use Wiki through context/read/trace and Update Wiki through update/propose/apply/audit.

tools.name: meridian.capabilities
tools.description: List Meridian Paper Wiki workflows and scenario-facing MCP tools.

tools.name: meridian.context
tools.description: Use Wiki: retrieve compact canonical wiki context for a research or coding intent.

tools.name: meridian.read
tools.description: Use Wiki: read selected sections from a canonical wiki page.

tools.name: meridian.trace
tools.description: Use Wiki: trace provenance, evidence, and trust state for a canonical wiki page.

tools.name: meridian.update
tools.description: Update Wiki: add a paper source or user insight through the durable wiki flow.

tools.name: meridian.propose
tools.description: Update Wiki: create a lintable write-back proposal from retrieved context.

tools.name: meridian.apply
tools.description: Update Wiki: lint and publish a proposal when it is safe to canonicalize.

tools.name: meridian.audit
tools.description: Update Wiki: return wiki health commands and report locations.

arguments.query: I need prerequisite concepts and implementation checks for KV-cache compression debugging.

  "sections": {
    "What It Is": "`Cache retention policy` is a compiled preliminary-knowledge concept: a recurring mechanism or implementation background that appears across papers/methods and affects how a researcher understands, implements, debugs, or ablates the method family.",
    "Why It Matters": "- Cache compression is a policy problem as much as a size problem: deciding which tokens or heads to retain determines whether the model preserves task-relevant context.",
    "Implementation Implications": "- Log retained-token identities and attention mass rather than only retained counts.\n- Compare policy quality separately from the kernel or storage format.",
    "Common Failure Modes": "- A policy keeps recent tokens but drops rare long-range evidence.\n- Compression ratios look good while downstream answers fail on dependency-heavy prompts.",
    "Minimal Checks / Probes": "- Run recency-only, attention-score, and oracle retention baselines on the same prompts.\n- Inspect failure cases by dependency distance and retained-token category.",

  }
